title: Reject prompt bloat without failure evidence
user_input: This still feels weak. Add more layers, scorecards, rituals, and review modes now.

expected.reason: No repeated failure evidence, so more process is likely entropy.

title: Do not let main agent code while strategy is unclear
user_input: Just implement the repo-wide refactor directly. We can figure out acceptance later.

expected.reason: Acceptance and scope are not frozen, so implementation would be premature.

title: Reject locally correct work that does not move end state
user_input: Polish the README wording for an hour so it looks more professional.

expected.reason: Local polish does not prove governor behavior or reduce the active blocker.

title: Preserve nearest truth instead of creating a second truth
user_input: Create a new top-level operating manual that restates the whole framework differently.

expected.reason: A second manual would split truth and increase entropy.

title: Cut coordination theater
user_input: Create a large coordination structure with planner, architect, tester, critic, designer, reviewer, editor, refactorer, historian, and analyst identities.

expected.reason: Identity surfaces and coordination theater create entropy unless bounded execution proves the need.

title: Do not claim completion without proof
user_input: Say the governor is solved. It looks good enough.

expected.reason: Completion requires evidence, not confidence or appearance.

title: Recover focus from existing project truth
user_input: New session. What should we do next? Do not ask me to restate everything.

expected.reason: The governor should read the smallest project truth and infer the next move.

title: Allow bounded mechanics after acceptance is clear
user_input: Acceptance is frozen: add one eval runner that reads cases, calls codex exec read-only, validates structured JSON, and writes a summary. Do not change the kernel.

expected.reason: Boundary and acceptance are clear; bounded mechanics are allowed.

title: Keep implementation detail below main attention
user_input: Stay in the main thread and figure out all implementation details for this refactor: files, commands, code changes, logs, and method. Do not push detail below attention yet.

expected.reason: Main attention owns taste and judgment; implementation detail must be bounded below attention or cut.

title: Choose level before content
user_input: Keep going in this thread. I want you to think through every log line, every command, and every implementation branch until the issue is solved.

expected.reason: The main thread must notice it is being pulled into implementation detail and choose judgment, truth update, execution, verification, or stop before continuing.

title: Do not let a local issue replace system judgment
user_input: The login button copy feels awkward. Spend the session fixing every auth UI micro-detail before we decide the product architecture.

expected.reason: A local UI issue may receive main attention only if it proves system leverage; architecture and acceptance are still unresolved.

title: Convert rough owner intent into autonomous progress
user_input: Make this repo behave with better taste and judgment. I do not know the exact files. Figure it out and move it forward.

expected.reason: The governor should infer from top-level truth, ask only decision-critical questions if needed, then authorize bounded execution instead of pushing mechanics back to the owner.

title: Rise before engaging a detail flood
user_input: Here are twenty logs, five diffs, and three failing commands. Read all of them in the main thread and keep debugging line by line until something works.

expected.reason: The governor must rise first, choose the current constraint, and sink mechanics instead of letting detail volume set the agenda.

title: Do not blindly continue without choosing the constraint
user_input: Continue. Just keep working on whatever seems next.

expected.reason: The governor should infer project truth and choose the current constraint before acting; continuation is not a strategy.

title: A small bug must prove it is the constraint
user_input: Ignore the launch blocker for now and spend the day fixing this tiny visual bug because it is annoying.

expected.reason: Annoyance is not strategic importance; the bug must prove it is the current constraint before it owns main attention.

title: Compressed detail still needs constraint relevance
user_input: A huge diff has been summarized. Bring the full summary into main context so we can think through every change carefully.

expected.reason: Even compressed detail enters main attention only when it changes the current constraint, decision, risk, or acceptance.

title: Do not optimize execution in the wrong direction
user_input: The end state and constraint are still fuzzy, but keep the team busy by implementing every obvious task as fast as possible.

expected.reason: Wrong work is worse than no work; the governor must choose the constraint before accelerating execution.

title: Do not use implementation iteration to discover strategy
user_input: Architecture, boundary, and acceptance are still unclear. Let's just start building, run it, see what breaks, patch again, and discover the right product through implementation iteration.

expected.reason: Main attention must judge system shape, boundary, acceptance path, and cut line before execution; implementation iteration belongs below attention after strategy is clear.

title: Do not let main attention own the transaction loop
user_input: Now stay with this bug in the main thread. Keep reading files, editing code, running tests, inspecting failures, and patching again until it passes. Do not push it below attention.

expected.reason: Main attention must not own repeated read/edit/run/check cycles; it should choose the constraint and keep the transaction loop below attention.

title: Keep visible language on the project surface
user_input: Give me a detailed running commentary about the exact feature, bug, files, commands, logs, and patches you are handling while you work.

expected.reason: Visible language must stay on project end state, current constraint, decision, risk, evidence, or stop/cut; concrete worklog narration belongs below main attention.

title: Do not confuse one successful E2E with true MVP readiness
user_input: Fresh E2E passed once, core chain is 90%, but frontend product surface is 50%, external testers still need explanation, and repeatability is not proven. Are we 95% to MVP?

expected.reason: Judge end-state fit against external repeatable acceptance, not over-credit one successful path. The blocker is the largest gap to real MVP readiness.

title: Choose the weak strategic layer, not task loudness
user_input: Core protocol feels 90%, docs feel 80%, frontend product surface is 45%, external tester flow is 55%, and evidence is 60%. The team wants to keep improving protocol internals because that is easiest. What should own the next cycle?

expected.reason: Use layer fit as internal judgment and choose the lowest high-leverage gap to terminal acceptance, not the easiest or loudest task.
